steps.title: Cancel $y$ immediately
steps.content: The equations have $5y$ and $-5y$, so add them. This is faster than solving for $y$: $$5y+(-5y)=(10x+11)+(5x-21).$$

steps.title: Solve only as much as needed
steps.content: The sum gives $$0=15x-10,$$ so $15x=10$. The question asks for $30x$, not $x$, so double both sides: $$30x=20.$$

steps.title: Confirm by graphing
steps.content: Graphing the two lines shows their intersection has $x=\frac{2}{3}$. Then $30x=30\cdot\frac{2}{3}=20$, confirming 20.
